# SECTION 3: Step-by-Step Derivations & Analytical Matrix

## 3.1 Analytical Matrix — Major P&ID Symbol Categories

Below is the **complete analytical breakdown** of every symbol category a KTU 2024 student must master, with derivations of identification logic.

### Category A: Instrument Bubble Identification Logic

**Step-by-step derivation of an instrument tag from a P&ID bubble:**

$$\text{Tag} = \underbrace{\text{First Letter}}_{\text{Measured Variable}} + \underbrace{\text{Subsequent Letters}}_{\text{Function(s)}} + \underbrace{\text{Loop Number}}_{\text{3-4 digit integer}}$$

**Worked Example — Decoding `TT-204` on a P&ID:**

$$\text{TT-204} \rightarrow \begin{cases} \text{First letter: T} \Rightarrow \text{Temperature (measured variable)} \\ \text{Second letter: T} \Rightarrow \text{Transmitter (function)} \\ \text{Loop No: 204} \Rightarrow \text{2nd area, 4th loop, in unit 200} \end{cases}$$

**Result:** Temperature Transmitter, Loop #204.

**Worked Example — Decoding `FV-101A`:**

$$\text{FV-101A} \rightarrow \begin{cases} \text{F} \Rightarrow \text{Flow} \\ \text{V} \Rightarrow \text{Valve} \\ \text{101A} \Rightarrow \text{Loop 101, unit A (redundant tag)} \end{cases}$$

**Result:** Flow Control Valve, Loop #101A.

### Category B: Valve Symbol Derivation Logic

A control valve symbol has **two parts**:
1. **Body symbol** (the basic valve type)
2. **Actuator symbol** (the part on top — the "head")

**General formula:**

$$\text{Valve Symbol} = \text{Body Type} + \text{Actuator Type} + \text{Failure Mode (if spring-return)}$$

| Valve Type | Symbol | ISA-5.1 Body |
|------------|--------|--------------|
| **Gate Valve** | Two triangles meeting at apex ▷◁ | Manual isolation |
| **Globe Valve** | Circle with disc ⨁ | Throttling |
| **Ball Valve** | Circle with solid ball ● | On/Off (full bore) |
| **Butterfly Valve** | Disc on stem | Large flow isolation |
| **Check Valve** | Circle with flap ⌐ | Backflow prevention |
| **Plug Valve** | Circle with rectangular plug | Slurry service |
| **Diaphragm Valve** | Bonnet with stem | Sanitary, corrosive |
| **Control Valve** | Globe body + actuator + stem | Modulating flow |
| **3-Way Valve** | Two inlets + one outlet | Diverting/mixing |
| **Pressure Safety Valve (PSV)** | Spring-loaded + body | Overpressure protection |

### Category C: Actuator Symbol Logic

| Actuator | Symbol | When Used |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| **Spring (diagonal)** | / | Fail-safe action; diaphragm/piston actuator |
| **Piston / Cylinder** | □ | High thrust; globe control valves |
| **Diaphragm** | ◡ | Modulating; low thrust |
| **Manual Handwheel** | ⌭ | Manual override |
| **Solenoid** | Ⓢ | On/Off ESD; fast acting |
| **Motor Actuator** | Ⓜ | Large valves, slow operation |

### Category D: Equipment Symbol Logic

| Equipment | Symbol | Functional Meaning |
|-----------|--------|--------------------|
| **Centrifugal Pump** | Ⓒ inside circle | Kinetic energy pump |
| **Positive Displacement Pump** | ⓅD | Volumetric pump |
| **Shell & Tube HX** | Shell with tubes | Heat exchange |
| **Horizontal Vessel** | Long ellipse | Storage/separator |
| **Vertical Vessel** | Tall ellipse | Column/reactor |
| **Tank (open top)** | Trapezoid | Atmospheric storage |
| **Compressor** | Ⓒ with arrow | Gas compression |
| **Turbine** | Triangular blades | Gas/steam expansion |

## 3.2 Exhaustive Derivation — ISA Suffix Letter Combination Rules

ISA-5.1 has **strict rules** for combining function letters. The derivations:

**Rule 1:** The first letter is the **measured/initiating variable**.

$$\text{Letter}_1 \in \{A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, W, X, Y, Z\}$$

**Rule 2:** Subsequent letters are **functions in order** (read like a sentence).

Example trace for a `FT-101`:

$$F \rightarrow T \rightarrow 101$$
$$\text{Flow} \rightarrow \text{Transmitter} \rightarrow \text{Loop 101}$$
$$\text{"Flow Transmitter 101"}$$

Example trace for a `PIRC-205`:

$$P \rightarrow I \rightarrow R \rightarrow C \rightarrow 205$$
$$\text{Pressure} \rightarrow \text{Indicate} \rightarrow \text{Record} \rightarrow \text{Control} \rightarrow \text{Loop 205}$$
$$\text{"Pressure Indicating Recording Controller, Loop 205"}$$

**Rule 3:** Modifier letters (D-differential, Q-integral, F-ratio) come **immediately after the first letter**.

Example: `FQRC-301` = Flow, **Ratio** (modifier), **Record** + **Control**, Loop 301.

**Rule 4:** A "T" appearing as second letter = **Transmitter**; as third letter = **Transducer**.

$$\text{TT-101} = \text{Temperature Transmitter}$$
$$\text{FIT-101} = \text{Flow Indicator Transmitter}$$

## 3.3 Analytical Matrix — Signal Type Identification

| Signal Line on P&ID | Engineering Meaning |
|---------------------|---------------------|
| Solid heavy line (—) | Process pipe, with size/material/rating annotation |
| Solid thin line | Process pipe, secondary |
| Dashed line (- - -) | Pneumatic (3-15 psi) signal OR electric signal |
| Dashed with double crosshatch | Hydraulic (1-100 psi) signal |
| Triple dashed | Capillary tube (for filled-bulb thermometers) |
| Dotted line | Internal software link inside DCS/PLC |
| Line with circle terminator | Electrical signal to/from device |

> [!NOTE]
> **Engineering Utility:** The signal type tells the engineer the **field wiring/instrumentation philosophy** — whether to run copper cables, pneumatic tubes, or fiber optics. This is critical in **Marshalling Cabinet** and **Junction Box** design.

# SECTION 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Question A1 (3 Marks) — Short Answer
**[KTU University Exam — Dec 2023]**

**Q:** Explain the significance of the **ISA-5.1 standard** in Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams. List any **four functional identification letters** with their meaning.

**Model Answer:**

The **ISA-5.1** standard is the universally accepted reference for P&ID symbols and instrument identification. It provides a **uniform language** that allows engineers, operators, and contractors to interpret a P&ID unambiguously. It standardizes three elements: **(i) instrument bubbles/circles, (ii) tag numbering, and (iii) signal line types.** Without ISA-5.1, plant documentation would be project-specific and could lead to **safety and operational failures**.

| Letter | Variable | Example Tag |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| **F** | Flow | FT-101 |
| **P** | Pressure | PT-201 |
| **L** | Level | LT-301 |
| **T** | Temperature | TT-401 |

**Valuation:** 1 mark for ISA significance, 2 marks for the table.

---

### Question A2 (3 Marks) — Short Answer
**[KTU University Exam — May 2024]**

**Q:** Differentiate between a **field-mounted instrument** and a **DCS-mounted instrument** on a P&ID. Draw both bubble symbols.

**Model Answer:**

| Parameter | Field-Mounted | DCS-Mounted |
|-----------|---------------|-------------|
| **Bubble symbol** | Plain circle (●) | Circle with a single horizontal line (⊖) |
| **Location** | On/near the process pipe or equipment | Inside the operator control room / DCS cabinet |
| **Example** | PT-101 (PT on the pipe) | PIC-101 (display on DCS screen) |
| **Output** | Typically a 4–20 mA signal sent to DCS | Control signal sent back to field valve |
| **Physical form** | Real device with housing, terminals | A "virtual" tag inside a controller card |

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Pitfall (A1 & A2):** Many students draw the bubble with a **stem** below it. A bubble with a stem is a **level-measuring instrument** (per ISA). A plain bubble = a field instrument. Always distinguish these carefully in the drawing.

## 5.2 Part B Questions (14 Marks Each) — Module Internal Choice

### Question A (14 Marks) — Option I
**[KTU University Exam — KTU 2024 Scheme, Model Paper Module 1]**

**Q (a)** With the help of a **neat sketch**, explain the **classification of control valves** based on the **body type** as per ISA-5.1. List at least **six valve body types** with their typical application. **(7 Marks)**

**Model Answer:**

Control valves on a P&ID consist of two parts: a **body** (the lower section) and an **actuator** (the upper section). The body type determines the flow characteristic and throttling behaviour. The standard ISA-5.1 valve body types are:

| # | Valve Type | Symbol (Body) | Typical Application |
|---|------------|---------------|---------------------|
| 1 | **Globe Valve** | Circle with disc/seat arrangement | Throttling / modulating control |
| 2 | **Ball Valve** | Circle with solid ball | On/Off isolation, clean fluids |
| 3 | **Butterfly Valve** | Disc on a stem | Large diameter lines, low pressure drop |
| 4 | **Gate Valve** | Two opposing triangles | Full-bore isolation (manual) |
| 5 | **Diaphragm Valve** | Bonnet with flexible diaphragm | Corrosive, hygienic, slurry service |
| 6 | **Plug Valve** | Cylindrical plug with port | Slurries, viscous fluids |
| 7 | **Check Valve** | Flap with hinge | Backflow prevention (one-way flow) |
| 8 | **3-Way Valve** | Two inlet + one outlet ports | Diverting or mixing service |

**Sketch of typical globe control valve with pneumatic diaphragm actuator:**

```
         Spring (/)
        [  Box  ]   ← Pneumatic Diaphragm Actuator
            |
            |  ← Stem
            |
          [ Stem Connector ]
            |
          /-\
         /   \          ← Valve Body
        | (o) |   ← Disc/Seat arrangement
         \   /
          \-/         ← Connected to process pipe
         __|__
        |     |     ← Process line connection
        |     |
        |_____|
```

---

**Q (b)** A process line has the following instruments marked on a P&ID: `FT-101`, `FIC-101`, `FCV-101`, `FY-101`, and `XV-101`. **(i)** Decode each tag, **(ii)** draw the loop diagram showing signal flow, and **(iii)** explain the function of each device. **(7 Marks)**

**Model Answer:**

**(i) Decoding the tags (ISA-5.1):**

| Tag | Decoding | Meaning |
|-----|----------|---------|
| **FT-101** | **F**(Flow) + **T**(Transmitter) | Flow Transmitter — measures flow and outputs 4–20 mA |
| **FIC-101** | **F**(Flow) + **I**(Indicate) + **C**(Control) | Flow Indicating Controller — DCS-mounted |
| **FCV-101** | **F**(Flow) + **CV**(Control Valve) | Flow Control Valve — final control element |
| **FY-101** | **F**(Flow) + **Y**(Relay/Compute) | Signal relay/splitter — computes or relays signal |
| **XV-101** | **X**(Unclassified) + **V**(Valve) | Solenoid/On-Off Valve (typically ESD) |

**(ii) Loop Diagram (Signal Flow):**

```
[Process Pipe]
      |
      |  (flow measured)
      v
   [FT-101]  ──── (4-20 mA) ────>  [FIC-101]
   Field                              DCS
                                       |
                                       | (control signal 3-15 psi / 4-20 mA)
                                       v
                                   [FY-101]
                                  Relay/compute
                                       |
                                       v
                                   [FCV-101]
                                   Final Control
                                       |
                                  [XV-101] (ESD override)
                                       |
                                       v
                                [Process Pipe]
```

**(iii) Function of each device:**

- **FT-101:** Senses flow (using orifice + DP cell, or magnetic flowmeter) and converts to standard signal.
- **FIC-101:** Compares measured flow with setpoint and outputs a corrective control signal.
- **FY-101:** A signal-conditioning device; may be a current-to-pressure (I/P) converter, square-root extractor, or signal splitter.
- **FCV-101:** Modulates flow by adjusting stem position based on controller output.
- **XV-101:** Solenoid valve used for **Emergency Shutdown (ESD)** — closes the line on safety demand, independent of the controller.

---

### Question B (14 Marks) — Option II (Internal Choice)
**[KTU University Exam — KTU 2024 Scheme Model Paper]**

**Q (a)** Explain the **ISA-5.1 instrument tag numbering system** in detail. Using a **step-by-step approach**, decode the following tags: `PDIT-201`, `LRC-303`, `TT-401A`, and `FQI-105`. **(7 Marks)**

**Model Answer:**

**The ISA-5.1 Tag Numbering System** has the following architecture:

$$\text{ISA Tag} = \underbrace{\text{1st Letter}}_{\text{Measured Variable}} + \underbrace{\text{2nd Letter}}_{\text{Modifier/Function}} + \underbrace{\text{3rd+ Letters}}_{\text{Functions (optional)}} + \underbrace{\text{Loop Number}}_{\text{Numeric identifier}}$$

**Decoding Steps:**

1. Identify the first letter → measured variable.
2. Identify modifier letters (D = Differential, Q = Integral/Ratio, F = Fraction).
3. Identify functional letters (I = Indicate, R = Record, C = Control, T = Transmit, S = Switch, V = Valve, A = Alarm, L = Low, H = High).
4. Identify the loop number (3-4 digit integer, area code often encoded).
5. Identify the suffix (A, B, C for redundant or parallel instruments).

**Decoded Tags:**

| Tag | 1st Letter | Modifier/Function | Loop | Full Meaning |
|-----|-----------|-------------------|------|--------------|
| **PDIT-201** | P = Pressure | D = Differential, I = Indicate, T = Transmit | 201 | **Differential Pressure Indicating Transmitter, Loop 201** |
| **LRC-303** | L = Level | R = Record, C = Control | 303 | **Level Recording Controller, Loop 303** |
| **TT-401A** | T = Temperature | T = Transmit | 401A | **Temperature Transmitter, Loop 401, Unit A** |
| **FQI-105** | F = Flow | Q = Ratio/Integral, I = Indicate | 105 | **Flow Ratio Indicating instrument, Loop 105** |

**Q (b)** Draw a **typical P&ID for a surge tank level control loop** including at least: one centrifugal pump, one horizontal vessel, one level transmitter (LT), one level controller (LIC), one level control valve (LCV), and a pressure safety valve (PSV). Identify and label **at least 6 P&ID symbols** in your drawing. **(7 Marks)**

**Model Answer:**

**P&ID Sketch Description:**

                                   +--------+
                                   | PSV-301|   [Spring-loaded]
                                   |   ↑    |   Safety Valve
                                   +---|----+
                                       |
   ============      =================================
   |   P-101   |     |     Horizontal Vessel V-101    |
   |  (Pump,   |     |                                 |
   |  Centri-  |     |   - - - - - - - - - - - - -    |
   |  fugal)   |====>|   L   T   -   3   0   1   -    |
   ============      |       ↑                         |
        ↑            |  [LT-301] Level Transmitter     |
        |            |       | (field-mounted)          |
        |            |       • (4-20 mA dashed line)    |
        |            |       ↓                          |
        |            |   [LIC-301] Level Indicating    |
        |            |   Controller (DCS-mounted) ⊖    |
        |            |       |                          |
        |            |       • (control signal)        |
        |            |       ↓                          |
        |            |   [LCV-301] Level Control       |
        |            |   Valve (Globe + Pneumatic      |
        |            |   Actuator)                      |
        |            +=================================+
